scenario: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — Ambiguous 'similar work' definition. Projects are industrial/factory construction, not residential/administrative buildings.

financials.ca_name: CA Girish Rao, M/s Rao & Partners

similar_works.project: Construction of Warehouse Complex for Food Corporation of India, Hassan
similar_works.notes: Warehouse complex — this is construction, but is it 'building construction (residential or commercial)' as defined in the tender? Warehouses are industrial/storage structures, not residential or commercial buildings.

similar_works.project: Cold Storage Facility for NAFED, Mysuru
similar_works.notes: Cold storage facility — specialised industrial structure. Not residential or commercial.
Factory building for a defence PSU. It's building construction for a PSU, but 'factory building' vs 'residential or commercial' is debatable.

similar_works.project: Workshop and Hangar at Air Force Station, Yelahanka
similar_works.notes: Workshop and hangar — specialised military structures. Building construction yes, but 'residential or commercial'? No.

experience.notes: All 4 projects are government, all above Rs. 2 Cr, all completed within 5 years, all have certificates. But NONE are clearly 'residential or commercial buildings' — they are warehouses, cold storage, factories, and hangars. The tender defines similar work as 'construction of buildings (residential or commercial)'. This is the ambiguity.

emd.in_favour_of: Commandant, Group Centre, CRPF, New Delhi

criteria.similar_works: NEEDS REVIEW — 4 projects, all government, all above Rs. 2 Cr, all with certificates. BUT none are clearly 'residential or commercial buildings' as defined in tender Section 5.1. They are warehouses, cold storage, factory, and hangar. The officer must decide whether these qualify as 'similar work'.

expected_verdict.notes: Tests semantic matching of 'similar work'. The LLM must understand the tender's definition ('construction of buildings, residential or commercial') and flag that warehouses/factories/hangars are a grey area. This is exactly the kind of qualitative judgment that needs human review.
